seoTitle: How to Accept a Pending Payment on PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to locate and manually approve an unclaimed or pending PayPal payment before it automatically returns to the sender.
intro: An incoming payment can sit in a pending or unclaimed state if your profile isn't fully set up or the currency doesn't match your account. This guide shows how to find and manually accept those funds before the automatic 30-day return kicks in. It's useful for anyone who has noticed money isn't landing in their balance right away.

steps.heading: Open your Activity feed
steps.body: Log in to PayPal, click Activity in the top menu, and scan your transaction rows for anything marked Pending or Unclaimed.

steps.heading: Open the transaction details
steps.body: Click the specific row to expand it and reveal the available action buttons.

steps.heading: Click Accept or Approve
steps.body: Select the Accept or Approve button to start the manual verification process for that payment.

steps.heading: Choose your currency option
steps.body: If the payment was sent in a different currency, decide whether to open a new balance in that currency or decline the transfer.

steps.heading: Submit to finalize
steps.body: Click Submit to confirm your choice and instantly claim the funds into your account.

faq.question: Why is my payment marked unclaimed instead of just pending?
faq.answer: Unclaimed usually means your profile is missing a confirmed email or phone number, so PayPal needs manual approval before releasing the funds.

faq.question: What happens if I don't accept the payment in time?
faq.answer: Unclaimed payments are typically returned to the sender automatically after about 30 days.

faq.question: Can I set up automatic acceptance for future payments?
faq.answer: Yes, once your account is fully verified you can configure preferences to accept certain currencies automatically.

faq.question: Will accepting a foreign currency payment cost extra?
faq.answer: Opening a new balance in a different currency may involve a conversion fee depending on your account settings.
